description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1489 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0021#AN0061 . The description of the approach follows: Adversary disables or stops critical services (e.g., Exchange, SQL, AV, endpoint monitoring) using native utilities or API calls, often preceding destructive actions (T1485, T1486). Behavioral chain: Elevated execution context + stop-service or sc.exe or ChangeServiceConfigW + terminated or disabled service + possible follow-up file manipulation.

external_references.name: MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1489: Service Stop

items.content: Establish continuous monitoring for service state changes on critical Windows services (e.g., Exchange, SQL Server, Antivirus, EDR agents).
Configure alerts for any service stop or disable commands issued via native utilities (sc.exe, net stop, Stop-Service) or direct API calls (ChangeServiceConfigW).
Correlate service termination events with process execution context to identify elevated privileges (e.g., SYSTEM, local admin).
Investigate the parent process chain and command-line arguments for suspicious patterns (e.g., mass service termination, use of automation scripts).
Monitor for subsequent destructive actions (T1485, T1486) such as file deletion, encryption, or overwriting following service disruption.
Isolate affected endpoints from the network to prevent lateral movement
Restore critical services to normal operation state
Collect forensic artifacts (process trees, command history, service logs) for analysis
Initiate incident response procedures for potential destructive attack
Review and update detection rules based on investigation findings to improve future coverage.
